rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE GRADY LOVETT 2011 REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Establish The Equitable Adoption Of Princess Green By Grady Lovett
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 06/22/2020 (no earlier date available, pursuant to court directive). The following issues must be addressed: 1) No courtesy copies of the attorney's declaration and memorandum of points and authorities have been provided. 2) No proposed orders have been provided. 3) According to the proof of service (filed 02/10/2020), notice of the hearing (Judicial Council form DE-120) was not served. Also, some beneficiaries entitled to specific cash gifts from the trust were not listed in the proof of service. 4) Has the subject real property been distributed outright to the petitioner? 5) The petitioner appears to be requesting the Court's determination of the equitable adoption to contest the San Francisco Assessment Appeals Board's denial of a parent-child transfer exemption from reassessment of the subject property. Why does this Court have jurisdiction to review the taxing authority's decision?

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARGARET BOYD
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/22/20 due to the Court's moratorium on personal appearances. Still no proposed order has been submitted, despite the issue having previously been raised. [LRSF 14.6.] Courtesy copies of filed papers since the 2/19/20 hearing have not been delivered to the assigned Examiner. [LRSF 2.7 B., 14.6 B. and C., and 14.93 G.] The notice of this hearing was mailed on 3/16/20, which was not at least 15 days' notice. Notice of the 6/22/20 hearing must be mailed to each person entitled to notice (and no one in care of another) at least 15 days before that hearing. Courtesy copies of all papers filed since the 2/19/20 hearing must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 6/15/20. The co-executors will also need to file an accounting for the first two years of estate administration soon, as they have well exceeded the statutory time for keeping an estate open. [LRSF 14.59 and Probate Code Section 12200.]

rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Establish Trust Terms, Petition For Order Approving Modification Of Trust Terms Under Pr
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/22/20 per court directive. (It is unclear why the surviving trustor/trustee does not simply fund the subtrusts in order to attain the same outcome she seeks by this petition, particularly in light of the consents of the remainder beneficiaries.)
